quick_facts.label: Award and named specialization
quick_facts.value: At NIPER S.A.S. Nagar, the relevant award is M.Tech. (Pharm.) in Pharmaceutical Technology (Process Chemistry); NIPER Hyderabad also lists M.Tech. (Pharm.) in Pharmaceutical Technology (Process Chemistry).

quick_facts.label: Typical duration
quick_facts.value: NIPER Hyderabad lists Pharmaceutical Technology (Process Chemistry) as a two-year M.Tech. (Pharm.) programme.

quick_facts.label: Entry route
quick_facts.value: Admissions to the NIPER Hyderabad M.Tech. (Pharm.) stream are through NIPER JEE; the national NIPER JEE 2026 portal covers M.Tech. admissions across the participating NIPERs.

quick_facts.label: Qualifying backgrounds
quick_facts.value: For Pharmaceutical Technology (Process Chemistry), NIPER Hyderabad’s ordinance specifies B.Pharm., M.Sc. in Organic Chemistry, or B.Tech. in Chemical Engineering as qualifying degrees; applicants must also satisfy the applicable NIPER JEE admission rules for their intake year.

quick_facts.label: Training focus
quick_facts.value: The process-chemistry pathway emphasizes translating synthetic routes from laboratory scale to kilogram-scale pilot plant and tonne-scale manufacturing, with a focus on efficient and environmentally friendlier API and intermediate processes.

sections.heading: Eligibility
paragraphs: For the NIPER Hyderabad M.Tech. (Pharm.) Pharmaceutical Technology (Process Chemistry) programme, the institute ordinance lists B.Pharm., M.Sc. (Organic Chemistry), and B.Tech. (Chemical Engineering) as qualifying degrees. Its admissions FAQ states a general minimum of 60% aggregate marks or a CGPA of 6.75/10 (or equivalent), calculated under the awarding institution’s norms. Candidates should treat the NIPER JEE information brochure for the relevant admission year as controlling, because programme, category, examination-score, and document requirements may be revised.
bullets: Eligible-degree examples: B.Pharm.; M.Sc. in Organic Chemistry; B.Tech. in Chemical Engineering.
General academic threshold stated by NIPER Hyderabad: 60% aggregate or CGPA 6.75/10, or equivalent; verify the current NIPER JEE brochure before applying.

sections.heading: Duration and mode
paragraphs: NIPER Hyderabad describes M.Tech. (Pharm.) in Pharmaceutical Technology (Process Chemistry) as a two-year programme. The institute’s department page further characterizes it as a two-year, full-time course.
bullets: Mode described by NIPER Hyderabad: full-time.

sections.heading: What the programme covers
paragraphs: This NIPER pathway is specifically framed as Pharmaceutical Technology (Process Chemistry), rather than a general engineering degree covering every pharmaceutical-technology area. NIPER Hyderabad says its purpose is to build practical capability in designing synthetic routes and methodologies that can move from laboratory work to pilot-plant and manufacturing scales.

The published teaching and research descriptions identify themes including organic-synthesis logic, industrial process and scale-up techniques, synthetic process chemistry, bulk-drug technologies, catalysis and biocatalysis, chiral-drug preparation, continuous or parallel processing, greener processes, and API/intermediate manufacture. Exact course titles, credit requirements, laboratory work, and project expectations should be checked against the admitting institute’s current course-of-study documents.
bullets: Scale-up of synthetic routes: laboratory to kilogram-scale pilot work and tonne-scale manufacturing.
Process R&D themes: efficiency, process economy, lower solvent use, catalysts/biocatalysts, continuous processing, and waste-minimising technologies.
Pharmaceutical application: active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs), key intermediates, and bulk-drug process technologies.

sections.heading: Admission through NIPER JEE
paragraphs: NIPER Hyderabad states that postgraduate admissions, including M.Tech. (Pharm.) Pharmaceutical Technology (Process Chemistry), are made through NIPER JEE. For the 2026 master’s cycle, the official NIPER JEE schedule recorded online applications from April 16, 2026, a final application deadline of May 23, 2026, and the computer-based test on June 13, 2026. The official portal subsequently posted master’s counselling notices, including web-option registration opening on July 10, 2026; these dates are historical 2026-cycle context, not a forecast for a later intake.
bullets: Use the official NIPER JEE portal for the current brochure, programme/seat matrix, merit and counselling notices.
Do not reuse 2026 dates for a future cycle; application, examination, counselling, eligibility and seat information can change year to year.

sections.heading: Further study and progression
paragraphs: A research-oriented next step is doctoral study. NIPER S.A.S. Nagar lists Pharmaceutical Technology (Process Chemistry) among its Ph.D. disciplines, and NIPER Hyderabad lists a four-year Ph.D. in Pharmaceutical Technology (Process Chemistry). Admission eligibility and availability for doctoral study are separate from the M.Tech. admission process and must be checked in the applicable Ph.D. notice.
bullets: Ph.D. progression in Pharmaceutical Technology (Process Chemistry) is available at NIPER campuses that offer the discipline.

For employment-oriented progression, the programme’s scale-up and process-development emphasis aligns with technical work involving API/intermediate process development and manufacturing technology; role requirements vary by employer.
